title: Add support for optimizing exists-forall problems.
body:
* Modifies smt2 backend to recognize `$anyconst` etc. assigned to a wire with the `maximize` or `minimize` attribute and emit a `; yosys-smt2-maximize` or `; yosys-smt2-minimize` directive as appropriate.
* Modifies `backends/smt2/smtbmc.py` and `smtio.py` to recognize those directives and emit a `(maximize ...)` or `(minimize ...)` command at the end of `smt_forall_assert()`, as described in the paper [νZ - An Optimizing SMT Solver](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/wp-content/uploads/2016/02/nbjorner-nuz.pdf) by Nikolaj Bjørner et al.
* Adds an example `examples/smtbmc/demo9.v` to show how it can be used.

AFAIK this is only supported by z3, but cvc4 might be able to do it, too.

Multiple objectives appear to be supported, but I have added an assertion to limit to only one `maximize` or `minimize` directive for now.  Unless someone else has a good use case for multiple objectives, I prefer to keep the assertion, as multiple objectives might very likely be a bug or user mistake.

Further improvements might include full weighted `assert-soft` constraint support.
